notes: Fault signature: pipeline-transformer panics on startup with 'proto: duplicate registration / incompatible protobuf runtime version' -> CrashLoopBackOff (pod_ready_replicas 3->0, pod_restart_total climbing). The diff of 8f1116b bumps google.golang.org/protobuf from v1.31.0 to v1.34.2 in pipeline-transformer/go.mod while the module still pins github.com/golang/protobuf v1.5.2 (indirect) and pkg/pb v0.18.0, which was generated against the v1.31 runtime; the v1.34 global type registry is incompatible with the legacy v1.5.2 registry, so init-time proto registration panics. That is the only diff that produces the observed protobuf runtime conflict. MISLEADING STRUCTURE (better-surface-match decoy via two dependency bumps): defc5b0 is ALSO a 'go module pins' commit on the SAME failing service (pipeline-transformer/go.mod) and is committed CLOSER to onset (14:48 vs 14:42), and it is the commit_sha recorded on the 14:50 pipeline-transformer deploy -- so 'blame the recent dependency bump / the deployed commit' points at defc5b0. But defc5b0's diff only bumps aws-sdk-go (v1.50->v1.53), which touches the S3 sink client and changes no protobuf/runtime dependency, so it cannot cause the registration panic. A third bump ac4d66a (aws-sdk-go-v2 patch) is in the window but on the root go.mod, not vendored by pipeline-transformer. The fix is to revert the protobuf pin in go.mod/go.sum (config-revert). pipeline-transformer is the first failing service; workflow-engine is blast radius (its pipeline-transformer endpoint returns connection refused while the pods crash-loop).
